phaseRuns.summary: Converted docs/specs/002-status-improvements.md to .ralphi/prd.json. Archived previous prd.json (ralph/sync-perf-remaining) to .ralphi/archive/2026-04-03-sync-perf-remaining/. Planned and wrote 4 right-sized user stories ordered by dependency: US-001 (sync_state.json data layer), US-002 (CLI flags + formatter + Config section), US-003 (Last Sync + Pending Files + Lock State sections), US-004 (Scheduler section + --verbose + porcelain + integration test). Branch: ralph/status-improvements.

Scanned chronicle (Rust, v0.5.0). All ralphi init artefacts were already in place: .ralphi/config.yaml (5 commands, 8 rules), AGENTS.md, prek.toml, and .gitignore entries. The only gap was the pre-commit hook — ran `prek install` to wire it up. Also committed 4 new feature specs (002–005) during this session, with the Windows support spec (005) marked draft pending JSONL separator research.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Add sync_state.json — schema, atomic write, and wire into sync/push/pull
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-001: created src/sync_state.rs with SyncState struct (last_sync_time, last_sync_duration_ms, last_sync_op), SyncOp enum (Sync/Push/Pull serialized as lowercase strings), sync_state_path() path helper, write_sync_state() atomic writer, and read_sync_state() reader returning Option<SyncState>. Registered module in lib.rs. Wired write_sync_state into sync_impl, push_impl, and pull_impl after lock acquisition (timing via Instant::now()) with non-fatal error logging. 9 unit tests cover all ACs. All 367 tests pass; clippy, fmt, and deny checks clean. Committed as feat(sync_state): US-001.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-001 fully implemented and committed. All acceptance criteria met. Three remaining stories (US-002, US-003, US-004) are open. US-002 and US-003 are unblocked; US-004 depends on both.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: Implement US-002: add --verbose, --porcelain, --no-color flags to StatusArgs; extract status_impl as testable function; implement StatusFormatter with ANSI color + TTY detection + NO_COLOR env; implement Config/Machine section. No new crate dependencies needed (use std::io::IsTerminal already in use, manual ANSI codes or termcolor if already transitively available).

phaseRuns.storyTitle: StatusArgs CLI flags, output formatter, and Config/Machine section
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-002: StatusArgs CLI flags, output formatter, and Config/Machine section. Added --verbose/-v, --porcelain, and --no-color flags to the Status command. Created StatusFormatter<W: io::Write> with ok/warn/err/kv methods and ANSI color support. Implemented should_use_color() with TTY detection and NO_COLOR env-var handling. Replaced the old status_impl with a new testable version backed by status_write() that captures output via a generic writer. Implemented emit_config_machine_section() covering machine name, remote URL, and per-agent sessions-dir existence checks. Added 13 new unit tests. All 380 unit + 8 integration tests pass, clippy/fmt/deny clean. Committed as feat(cli): [US-002].
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-002 fully satisfied all 7 acceptance criteria. StatusFormatter uses a generic writer for test isolation (no process spawning). Manual ANSI codes avoided adding any new dependency. The `status_write` private inner function is accessible from mod tests because Rust child modules can access parent private items. The config::Config re-export means function signatures use config::Config directly. 13 tests added covering all required test scenarios.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: US-003: Last Sync, Pending Files, and Lock State sections. Implement three data-driven sections in status_write: (1) read sync_state.json (from US-001) and display timestamp+duration or ⚠ never; (2) use scan module to count pending files; (3) read chronicle.lock and determine free/held/stale state. Build on StatusFormatter and status_write established in US-002.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Last Sync, Pending Files, and Lock State sections
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-003: Last Sync, Pending Files, and Lock State sections of `chronicle status`. Added three new private emit functions (emit_last_sync_section, emit_pending_files_section, emit_lock_state_section), wired them into status_write, and added 10 unit tests covering all acceptance criteria. All 390 tests pass; clippy, fmt, and cargo deny clean. Committed as feat(cli): US-003.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-003 fully implemented and tested. Three data-driven status sections added: Last Sync (reads sync_state.json), Pending Files (uses scan module read-only against state cache), Lock State (reads chronicle.lock, checks PID liveness and age). 10 tests cover all acceptance criteria. One remaining story: US-004 (Scheduler section, --verbose expansion, porcelain completion, integration test).
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: Implement US-004: Scheduler section (crontab installed/not-installed/malformed, next-run estimate), --verbose Pending Files (full file list), --verbose Config (effective values), all remaining porcelain keys, integration test for happy-path and error-path. Check scheduler/cron.rs for existing cron expression parsing before adding cron math.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Scheduler section, --verbose expansion, porcelain completion, and integration test
phaseRuns.summary: Implemented US-004 — the final story in the chronicle status improvements PRD. Added Scheduler section (installed/not_installed/malformed with next-run time computation), --verbose expansion for Pending Files (file paths) and Config (effective values), completed all porcelain keys from spec §3.3, and added 19 new tests (16 unit + 3 integration). All 417 tests pass; clippy, fmt, and deny all clean. All 4 PRD stories are now done.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-004 implemented cleanly. All acceptance criteria met: scheduler section with 3 states, next-run time via modulo on Unix timestamp, verbose file-path listing, verbose config effective values, all porcelain keys emitted in every code path (including empty values on config error), exit-0 guarantee tested, and 3 integration tests in tests/integration.rs covering happy path, porcelain happy path, and missing-sessions-dir error path. No scope creep. No new dependencies added.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: All PRD user stories are complete (US-001 through US-004). The loop is done.

phaseRuns.summary: Converted docs/specs/003-l3-canonicalization-hardening.md to .ralphi/prd.json. Archived the previous PRD (ralph/status-improvements) to .ralphi/archive/2026-04-03-status-improvements/prd.json. Planned and wrote 3 right-sized user stories on branch ralph/l3-canon-hardening: US-001 (expand all proptest generators — home/subpath components, content templates, nested JSON, array-of-strings; src/canon test-only), US-002 (cargo-fuzz workspace, fuzz_roundtrip libFuzzer target, seed corpus), US-003 (CI integration: fuzz build-only step in ci.yml + scheduled fuzz.yml for GitHub and Forgejo). Dependency chain: US-001 → US-002 → US-003.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Expand proptest generators — richer home/subpath, content templates, nested JSON, and array tests
phaseRuns.summary: US-001 complete. Expanded proptest generators in src/canon/levels.rs: arb_subpath() now covers plain/space/dot components up to 8 deep; new arb_home_path() covers plain/dot/hyphen/space usernames with /Users and /home roots; added prop_l3_content_templates (6 templates), roundtrip_deeply_nested_json (4-level deep JSON), and roundtrip_array_of_strings (1-5 home paths in array). All 409 tests pass; clippy/fmt/deny clean. Commit 3d4e3cc.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-001 fully satisfies all acceptance criteria. Only #[cfg(test)] code was touched. The two remaining open stories (US-002: cargo-fuzz workspace, US-003: CI integration) are unblocked and ready to proceed.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: Pick up US-002: create fuzz/ sub-workspace with Cargo.toml (libfuzzer-sys, arbitrary, chronicle path dep), implement fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_roundtrip.rs (extract home/level from bytes, assert round-trip), and create at least 2 seed corpus files in fuzz/corpus/fuzz_roundtrip/.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: Cargo-fuzz workspace, fuzz_roundtrip target, and seed corpus
phaseRuns.summary:
Implemented US-002: Cargo-fuzz workspace, fuzz_roundtrip target, and seed corpus.

Created the fuzz/ sub-workspace with:
- fuzz/Cargo.toml: standalone workspace with libfuzzer-sys + serde_json in [dependencies], chronicle via path, arbitrary in [dev-dependencies], [workspace] declaration
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_roundtrip.rs: libFuzzer target interpreting raw bytes as (home_path_len_byte, level_byte, home_path, json_line); builds TokenRegistry; asserts the round-trip invariant for double-normalised JSON objects; handles all edge cases without panicking
- fuzz/corpus/fuzz_roundtrip/seed_simple.bin: L2 seed with /Users/testuser/Dev/project
- fuzz/corpus/fuzz_roundtrip/seed_claude.bin: L3 seed with /Users/testuser
- fuzz/.gitignore: excludes target/ and artifacts/

Two correctness issues found and fixed through live fuzzing: (1) serde_json BTreeMap key ordering requires pre-normalisation; (2) serde_json float non-idempotency requires double-normalisation guard + JSON-objects-only filter.

Verified: cargo +nightly fuzz build passes; 10-second run produces zero crashes (~1M executions); all 409 root tests pass; clippy/fmt/deny clean. Commit: 7782293.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: US-002 fully delivered. The fuzz workspace compiles and runs cleanly. Two non-obvious serde_json serialisation behaviours were discovered via live fuzzing and correctly handled in the fuzz target. US-003 (CI integration) remains open and is the next story to implement.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: Implement US-003: add fuzz-build step to ci.yml and forgejo ci.yml, create .github/workflows/fuzz.yml and .forgejo/workflows/fuzz.yml for scheduled weekly fuzzing. Must carefully match existing job/step style in each CI file and use the nightly toolchain.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: CI integration — fuzz build-only step in ci.yml and scheduled fuzz.yml workflow
phaseRuns.summary: US-003 complete: added fuzz-build job to .github/workflows/ci.yml and .forgejo/workflows/ci.yml (nightly toolchain, cargo +nightly fuzz build fuzz_roundtrip, build-only); created .github/workflows/fuzz.yml and .forgejo/workflows/fuzz.yml as weekly scheduled workflows (cron '0 2 * * 0') that run the fuzz target for 60 seconds and fail automatically on any crash. All quality checks pass. All 3 PRD stories are now done.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: All 3 user stories in the ralph/l3-canon-hardening PRD are now done. US-001 expanded proptest generators; US-002 created the cargo-fuzz workspace and seed corpus; US-003 wired CI integration with a build-only fuzz step on every push and a weekly 60-second fuzzer run. No scope drift, no outstanding risks.

phaseRuns.summary: Converted docs/specs/004-doctor-command.md to .ralphi/prd.json. Archived the previous PRD (ralph/l3-canon-hardening) to .ralphi/archive/2026-04-03-l3-canon-hardening/prd.json. Planned and wrote 3 right-sized user stories on branch ralph/doctor-command: US-001 (CheckResult/CheckState types + all four check functions with unit tests), US-002 (DoctorArgs CLI wiring + human-readable formatter + exit codes), US-003 (--porcelain output + integration tests). Linear dependency chain: US-001 → US-002 → US-003.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: CheckResult / CheckState types and all four check functions
phaseRuns.summary:
Implemented US-001: CheckResult/CheckState types and all four check functions.

Created src/doctor/mod.rs (~950 lines) with:
- CheckState enum (Pass, Warn, Error, Skipped)
- CheckResult struct (key, state, detail, hint) with concise constructor helpers
- check_config(config_path) → 3 results: config.file, config.toml, config.remote
- check_git(repo_path, remote_url, ssh_key_paths, check_remote closure) → 3 results with 5s TCP timeout
- check_agents(pi_enabled, pi_dir, claude_enabled, claude_dir) → 2 results with JSONL file count
- check_scheduler(crontab_lines, lock_path, lock_timeout_secs) → 2 results for cron and lock state
- 23 unit tests covering all pass/warn/error/skipped branches
- default_check_remote() and default_ssh_key_paths() for production use

All AC satisfied. Quality checks pass (432 tests with --test-threads=1; pre-existing flaky test push_merges_new_entries_with_existing_repo_content fails under parallel execution but passes in isolation). Committed as feat(doctor): US-001.
phaseRuns.reflectionSummary: All 10 acceptance criteria satisfied. The module was placed directly in src/doctor/mod.rs (extracted from cli/mod.rs since check logic + tests exceeded the ~300-line threshold mentioned in the notes). The injectable closure pattern for check_remote cleanly enables unit testing of all network branches without a real TCP connection. 23 unit tests pass. Code style and lints are clean.
phaseRuns.nextIterationPlan: US-002: Wire chronicle doctor into the CLI. Add DoctorArgs { porcelain, no_color }, Doctor subcommand in main.rs/cli/mod.rs, implement doctor_impl with grouped output (Config/Git/Agents/Scheduler sections), ✓/⚠/✗ symbols, remediation hints, and summary line. Enforce exit codes 0/1/2. Reuse StatusFormatter<W> or add DoctorFormatter. Unit tests for output shapes.

phaseRuns.storyTitle: DoctorArgs CLI wiring and human-readable output formatter
